title: Pyrus albovii
taxonomy: Pyrus albovii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Pyrus, which includes other species of pear trees. Pyrus albovii is native to the Caucasus region. Within the genus Pyrus, Pyrus albovii is a distinct species. Further classification includes it being a member of the subgenus Pyrus.

intro: Pyrus albovii, also known as Caucasian pear, is a species of pear tree native to the Caucasus region. The health benefits of Pyrus albovii include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Pyrus albovii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
